title: centaurea movlavia
intro: Centaurea movlavia, a flowering plant species, is native to the Balkan Peninsula and is also known as Balkan cornflower or Balkan knapweed. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Centaurea movlavia include its potential to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Herbal preparations of Centaurea movlavia can be found in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
